title: Sunset wave in Nazaré taken with our drone
creator: lido_wave (Instagram, verified account)
notes: COASTAL-LANDSCAPE EXEMPLAR (subgenre: coastal, ocean, and sea-cliff landscapes) -- added following the same per-record verification rigor as this pack's original 10 entries (see e.g. bbc-earth-aerial.json, skypixel-landscape.json). Independently re-verified this session via WebFetch of the live Instagram post (https://www.instagram.com/lido_wave/reel/DDKrINJo7mI/), which returned: account username 'lido_wave' with a verified badge; caption text "Sunset wave in Nazare' taken with our drone \U0001F919" (a drone shot of a large breaking wave at sunset near Nazare, Portugal, beneath the town's famous sea-cliff/lighthouse point); hashtags #bigwave #surfing #surf #djiglobal #djimavic3; engagement of 115 likes and 3 comments; and a post date of 2024-12-04. This matches, field for field, the prior agent's candidate record (same caption text, same hashtag set, same 115/3 engagement figures, same verified-account status), so the source is treated as confirmed in this session rather than merely trusted from the candidate JSON. No Creative Commons, CC0, or other explicit license marking was present anywhere in the fetched content, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `license_category` is set to 'all-rights-reserved' -- this determination method is a direct content fetch of the live post in this session, not an inference from account type or content genre. Consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no image/video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its prohibition on retaining all-rights-reserved footage. `platform` is set to 'other' (Instagram is not YouTube or Vimeo, the two named platforms in the schema/spec enum). `duration` is left null: no oEmbed/API call returning a runtime was available for this Instagram post in this session (Instagram's oEmbed endpoint requires an authenticated Meta Graph API app token not available here, and no browser-automation/playback tool was invoked to time the clip), so rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number this field stays unset. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is a branded/personal account's Instagram reel, not a formally judged contest or broadcaster showcase entry, and no award or festival selection was found or claimed anywhere in the fetched content -- inventing one would violate the no-fabrication constraint. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing 0.72-0.88 range: sharpness=0.76 (the #djimavic3 hashtag confirms a capable prosumer drone/camera platform, a positive signal, offset by Instagram's aggressive video re-compression, which measurably degrades perceived per-frame detail relative to a broadcaster/festival master file); exposure=0.70 (sunset backlit conditions -- a bright sky and dark, spray-lit wave face in the same frame -- are a genuinely harder dynamic-range case than the flat/well-lit daylight conditions of several other exemplars in this pack, and no HDR/log-profile grading claim was found to offset that); motion_smoothness=0.78 (a single continuous hovering/tracking shot of one wave event, plausibly gimbal-stabilized given the drone class, but with no explicit stabilization-software or gimbal-mode claim verified in this session, so scored near this pack's median rather than at its high end). `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.76, exposure=0.70, motion_smoothness=0.78, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3 -c "..."` -- note that `default_weights()` with no arguments now returns the 'default-v2' profile (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25 each), not the 'default-v1' profile the pack's original 10 records cite, reflecting that this repo's weights.py has since added a Milestone-2 composition-scoring profile and changed its module-wide default; however, since `composition` is None here, `compute_composite_score` skips the zero-information composition slot and renormalizes over only the three populated signals, so the arithmetic result is identical to an equal 1/3-each weighting regardless of which profile is used -- confirmed: (0.76+0.70+0.78)/3 = 0.7466666..., matching the function's actual return value exactly (0.7466666666666667). Rounded to 0.7467 here. `editorial_style` reflects a genuine attempt, not a shortcut: this session made one live WebFetch call against the Instagram post URL, which returned only caption text, hashtag list, verified-badge status, and like/comment counts -- no cut count, shot-length, transition-style, or pacing description of any kind was present in that response, and no additional web search for behind-the-scenes/editing commentary on this specific reel was performed in this session (a prior agent's WebSearch attempt at this already returned only unrelated general Nazare big-wave coverage from outlets such as Surfer/The Inertia/dronedj, none describing this reel's own edit). No browser-automation/playback tool (e.g. claude-in-chrome) was invoked in this session to scrub or watch the actual video, so `review_method` stays 'not_reviewed' and all quantitative editorial fields (format, estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, transition_styles_observed) stay null/empty rather than being guessed from the reel's likely genre.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Live playback review was not performed in this session: no browser-automation/video playback tool (e.g. claude-in-chrome) was invoked to scrub or watch this reel. A WebFetch of the live Instagram post URL retrieved only caption text ("Sunset wave in Nazare' taken with our drone"), hashtags (#bigwave #surfing #surf #djiglobal #djimavic3), verified-badge status, and engagement counts (115 likes, 3 comments) -- no cut count, shot length, transition style, or pacing description of any kind. No further web search for editing/behind-the-scenes commentary on this specific reel was performed in this session. Given the format is a single native Instagram Reel URL (vertical short-form), it is plausibly a single continuous take, but this is an inference from platform/URL convention, not an observation, so `format` is left null rather than asserted as 'short-form-reel' without an actual review.
